seoTitle: How to Create Mockups in Canva for Etsy
metaDescription: Learn how to create product mockups in Canva for your Etsy shop, turning flat designs into realistic images that boost sales.
intro: Realistic mockups help Etsy shoppers visualize a product before buying, and Canva's mockup templates make it possible to create these images without a photo studio. This guide covers building product mockups suited for an Etsy listing.

steps.heading: Search for mockup templates or frames
steps.body: Look for mockup graphics in Canva's Elements panel that match your product type, such as mugs, shirts, or wall art.

steps.heading: Insert your design into the mockup
steps.body: Drag your artwork into the mockup's placeholder or frame area so it displays on the product surface.

steps.heading: Adjust positioning and scale
steps.body: Resize and reposition your design so it fits naturally within the mockup's printable area.

steps.heading: Add branding elements around the shot
steps.body: Include a background color, logo, or props that match your shop's overall aesthetic.

steps.heading: Export for your Etsy listing
steps.body: Download the finished mockup at a size appropriate for Etsy's listing image requirements.

faq.question: Are mockup templates free in Canva?
faq.answer: Many are free, though some higher-quality or branded mockup sets are part of Canva Pro.

faq.question: Can I use the same mockup for multiple product designs?
faq.answer: Yes, just swap out the artwork inside the mockup frame for each new product.

faq.question: What image size does Etsy recommend for listings?
faq.answer: Etsy recommends at least 2000px on the shortest side, which Canva can export at directly.

faq.question: Do I need real product photos instead of mockups?
faq.answer: Mockups are a good starting point, but many sellers add real photos alongside them for extra buyer confidence.
